scenario: Lattice Bank books loan terms expressed as 2 years 6 months across 12000 loan instances in Camunda 8.8 and needs a FEEL value type that represents this calendar-based interval cleanly so that DMN tables and FEEL expressions can reason about months and years without converting to seconds.
question: Which FEEL data type represents a calendar-based interval of years and months for the loan term?

options.text: Days-time-duration value with format PxDTxHxMxS, backed by java.time.Duration and used to express seconds-based intervals
Years-months-duration value with format PxYxM, backed by java.time.Period and used to express calendar-based intervals
Date-time value with format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss, backed by java.time.LocalDateTime for absolute timestamps
Date value with format yyyy-MM-dd, backed by java.time.LocalDate and used to express a calendar day with no time part

a.text: Incorrect. Days-time-duration uses the format PxDTxHxMxS and is backed by java.time.Duration; it expresses seconds-based intervals, not calendar-based years and months.

b.text: Correct. Years-months-duration uses the format PxYxM and is backed by java.time.Period; it is the FEEL type that expresses calendar-based intervals of years and months, such as P2Y6M.

c.text: Incorrect. Date-time represents an absolute instant with format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss; it is a point in time, not an interval span of years and months.

d.text: Incorrect. Date represents a single calendar day with format yyyy-MM-dd backed by java.time.LocalDate; it has no notion of an interval length.

explanation: FEEL defines years-months-duration with format PxYxM, backed by java.time.Period, for calendar-based spans expressed in years and months; days-time-duration is for seconds-based intervals and date or date-time represent points in time, not durations.
